title: Static Site Scaffolding and Visual Iteration

workflow_patterns.title: Plan Mode for Project Scaffolding
workflow_patterns.description: User requests a feature, agent enters plan mode, explores the directory, launches a Plan sub-agent, writes a detailed implementation plan, and exits for user approval before coding.

workflow_patterns.title: Parallel Multi-File Creation
workflow_patterns.description: Agent creates multiple CSS and JS files in parallel batches, following a predefined directory layout from the plan. Files include design tokens, base styles, layout, components, animations, data, and logic.

workflow_patterns.title: Screenshot-Feedback-Fix Cycle
workflow_patterns.description: Agent deploys site locally, takes Playwright screenshot, user reviews and reports issues (wrong images, broken modal), agent fixes and re-screenshots to verify.

workflow_patterns.title: Asset Discovery via Sub-Agent Research
workflow_patterns.description: Agent delegates image URL research to a general-purpose sub-agent that performs web searches, then integrates results back into source files via targeted edits.

workflow_patterns.title: Iterative Bug Fix from User Report
workflow_patterns.description: User reports a UI bug (modal close button not working), agent reads source code, identifies missing event listener, applies targeted fix, and confirms resolution.

creations.name: static-site-scaffold
creations.description: Scaffold a static HTML/CSS/JS website from a specification. Trigger: 'build a static site', 'create a website', 'scaffold a project'.
creations.skill_md_content:
---
description: Scaffold a static HTML/CSS/JS website from a specification. Trigger: 'build a static site', 'create a website', 'scaffold a project'.
tags: [frontend, scaffolding, html, css, static-site]
allowed_tools: [Write, Bash, Read, Edit]
---

# Static Site Scaffold

Generate a complete static website project from a brief specification. Produces a structured set of files following modern CSS architecture with design tokens, responsive grid layout, and vanilla JS interactivity.

## Directory Structure

Always create this standard layout:
```
project/
├── index.html
├── css/
│   ├── variables.css    # Design tokens: colors, spacing, typography, shadows
│   ├── base.css         # Reset, body, typography defaults
│   ├── layout.css       # Header, grid, footer, responsive breakpoints
│   ├── components.css   # Cards, modals, search, filters, badges
│   └── animations.css   # Keyframes, hover transitions, entrance effects
├── js/
│   ├── data.js          # Sample data as a const array
│   └── app.js           # Rendering, filtering, search, modal, event delegation
└── favicon.svg          # Simple SVG icon matching the theme
```

## Step 1: Define Design Tokens

Start with `css/variables.css`. Extract ALL magic values into CSS custom properties:

```css
:root {
  /* Colors */
  --color-bg-primary: #1a1a2e;
  --color-bg-card: #16213e;
  --color-accent: #e94560;
  --color-text: #eee;
  --color-text-muted: #aaa;

  /* Typography */
  --font-heading: 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif;
  --font-body: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;

  /* Spacing */
  --space-xs: 0.25rem;
  --space-sm: 0.5rem;
  --space-md: 1rem;
  --space-lg: 2rem;
  --space-xl: 3rem;

  /* Shadows */
  --shadow-card: 0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
  --shadow-hover: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
}
```

Never hardcode colors, fonts, or spacing in other CSS files. Always reference variables.

## Step 2: Create Base Styles

`css/base.css` provides the reset and global typography:
- Box-sizing border-box on all elements
- Zero margin/padding reset
- Body font, background, and text color from variables
- Smooth scrolling
- Custom scrollbar styling for dark themes

## Step 3: Build Layout

`css/layout.css` handles structural positioning:
- Sticky header with gradient or glass-morphism
- CSS Grid for card container: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr))`
- Footer with muted text
- Single responsive breakpoint for mobile adjustments

## Step 4: Style Components

`css/components.css` defines reusable UI pieces:
- **Cards**: image with hover zoom, gradient overlay, text section
- **Modal**: fixed overlay, scrollable content, close button with hover
- **Search input**: full-width, styled for dark theme
- **Filter pills**: horizontal scrollable row, active state with accent color
- **Badges**: glass-morphism or semi-transparent background

## Step 5: Add Animations

`css/animations.css` defines entrance and interaction effects:
- `fadeInUp` keyframe for card entrance with staggered delay
- `shimmer` keyframe for loading placeholders
- Hover transitions on cards (scale, shadow, overlay shift)
- Modal fade-in/scale transition

## Step 6: Write Sample Data

`js/data.js` contains 6-10 sample items as a `const` array of objects. Each object has:
- `id` (number)
- `title` (string)
- `description` (string)
- `category` (string from 3-4 categories)
- `image` (URL from picsum.photos or unsplash with seeded IDs for consistency)
- Domain-specific fields (e.g., `ingredients`, `duration`, `difficulty`)

## Step 7: Write Application Logic

`js/app.js` handles all interactivity with event delegation:
- **Render function**: builds card HTML from data array, inserts into grid container
- **Search**: debounced input handler filtering by title, description, and detail fields
- **Category filter**: pill click toggles active category, re-renders filtered cards
- **Modal**: click card to open detail view, close via X button, overlay click, or Escape key
- **Accessibility**: `aria-live` on grid, focus trapping in modal

## Step 8: Assemble HTML

`index.html` ties everything together:
- Google Fonts link for heading + body fonts
- CSS imports in order: variables, base, layout, components, animations
- Semantic HTML: `<header>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`
- JS imports with `defer`: data first, then app
- Empty `<div id="modal">` for dynamic modal content

## Step 9: Create Favicon

Simple SVG icon (under 20 lines) matching the project theme.

## Verification

```bash
# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8080 &

# Verify all files exist
find project/ -type f | sort

# Check no broken CSS variable references
grep -r 'var(--' project/css/ | grep -v ':root'
```

Open http://localhost:8080 and verify:
- Cards render with images
- Search filters correctly
- Category pills work
- Modal opens and closes (X, overlay, Escape)
- Responsive at narrow widths
creations.rationale:
Repeatable 9-file creation pattern detected across sessions.
- Consistent CSS architecture (variables, base, layout, components, animations)
- Eliminates manual file-by-file creation with a single scaffold command

creations.name: visual-review-loop
creations.description: Deploy locally and iterate with Playwright screenshots. Trigger: 'review the site', 'take a screenshot', 'visual check'.
creations.skill_md_content:
---
description: Deploy locally and iterate with Playwright screenshots. Trigger: 'review the site', 'take a screenshot', 'visual check'.
tags: [testing, visual-review, playwright, iteration, frontend]
allowed_tools: [Bash, Read, Edit, Write, AskUserQuestion]
---

# Visual Review Loop

Structured workflow for iterating on web UI through screenshot capture, user feedback, and targeted fixes.

## Step 1: Deploy Locally

Start a local HTTP server if not already running:
```bash
python3 -m http.server 8080 &
```

Verify the server is responsive:
```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:8080
```

## Step 2: Capture Screenshot

Write a Playwright script to capture the current state:
```python
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

with sync_playwright() as p:
    browser = p.chromium.launch()
    page = browser.new_page(viewport={'width': 1440, 'height': 900})
    page.goto('http://localhost:8080', wait_until='networkidle')
    # Wait for images and animations to settle
    page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    page.screenshot(path='/tmp/review-screenshot.png', full_page=True)
    browser.close()
```

Show the screenshot to the user using the Read tool on the image file.

## Step 3: Collect Feedback

Present the screenshot and ask the user what needs to change. Common feedback categories:
- **Content issues**: wrong images, placeholder text, missing data
- **Layout issues**: alignment, spacing, overflow, responsive problems
- **Interaction bugs**: buttons not working, modal stuck, scroll issues
- **Style issues**: colors, fonts, contrast, hover states

## Step 4: Apply Fixes

For each piece of feedback:
1. Read the relevant source file
2. Identify the specific code that needs to change
3. Apply a targeted edit (not a full rewrite)
4. If fixing asset issues (images), research correct URLs via web search

## Step 5: Re-capture and Verify

Run the Playwright script again to capture the updated state. Show the new screenshot to the user.

Repeat steps 3-5 until the user is satisfied.

## Pre-Deployment Checklist

Before the first screenshot, verify these common issues:
- [ ] All event listeners attached (modal close, form submit, button click)
- [ ] Images have alt text and proper src URLs
- [ ] No console errors (check with `page.on('console', ...)`)
- [ ] Responsive at 375px, 768px, and 1440px widths
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works (Tab, Escape, Enter)
creations.rationale:
Screenshot-feedback-fix cycle repeated twice in the session.
- Pre-deployment checklist would have caught the modal close bug
- Structured iteration loop reduces per-cycle overhead
addressed_patterns: Iterative Bug Fix from User Report

summary:
Two strong skill candidates emerged from the recipe site build.
- Static-site-scaffold eliminates manual 9-file creation with consistent project structures
- Visual-review-loop formalizes deploy-screenshot-feedback-fix with a pre-deployment checklist
- 5 workflow patterns detected: scaffolding, file creation, screenshot loops, research, bug fixing
user_profile: Frontend-focused developer building static websites. Relies on plan mode for project scoping, iterates through visual screenshots, and expects polished design with curated imagery. Values working demos over documentation.
